mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in industrial production signals expanding real economic output, which lifts corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are negatively correlated with INDPRO month-over-month changes over any consecutive 3-year window (Pearson correlation > -0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 6.0%, it signals a weakening labor market that depresses consumer spending and earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: In more than 50% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 6.0, the S&P 500 60-day forward return is positive (contradicting the negative prediction).

mechanism_narrative: A negative real interest rate, defined as the federal funds rate minus CPI inflation, indicates highly accommodative monetary policy that compresses discount rates and boosts equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The 12-month rolling rank IC of the real rate signal (FEDFUNDS minus CPIAUCSL inflation) for 20-day forward returns remains below 0.03 over any 2-year period.

mechanism_narrative: During the early stage of an economic expansion, strong payroll growth signals rising corporate earnings and boosts equity markets, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In periods identified as early expansion (GDP trend > 2% and unemployment declining), the 20-day forward return following strong payroll growth is not significantly higher than the unconditional mean (one-sided t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly drop in housing starts below 1,000,000 units signals a sharp contraction in residential investment and consumer confidence, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as markets quickly price in demand weakness.
failure_condition: When HOUST falls below 1000, the average 5-day forward return of the S&P 500 is positive across all such events in the sample.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in nonfarm payrolls and real retail sales creates a virtuous cycle of rising income and consumption, amplifying corporate earnings and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term (PAYEMS change * RSAFS change) has no significant positive coefficient in predicting 20-day forward S&P 500 returns (linear regression p-value > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A rising real GDP trend, reflecting sustained aggregate demand growth, underpins long-term corporate earnings expansion and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The 4-quarter rolling correlation between GDPC1 year-over-year change and subsequent 60-day S&P 500 return is negative over any 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, rising federal funds rates increase discount rates and borrowing costs, compressing equity valuations and leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In periods where the Federal Reserve has raised the federal funds rate in at least two consecutive FOMC meetings, the average 20-day forward S&P 500 return is positive.
